│ ▲ Detected TypeScript < 4.9 or incompatible tooling, populating with │ JavaScript examples │ │ # Storybook Setup │ ## Project Info │ | Property | Value | │ |----------|-------| │ | Version | 0.0.0-pr-34612-sha-d8de7419 | │ | Renderer | @storybook/react | │ | Framework | @storybook/react-vite | │ | Builder | @storybook/builder-vite | │ | Config Dir | `.storybook` | │ | CSF Format | CSF3 | │ | Addons | @chromatic-com/storybook, @storybook/addon-vitest, │ @storybook/addon-a11y, @storybook/addon-docs, │ @storybook/addon-onboarding | │ Attention: The following instructions must be followed in order to │ successfully set up Storybook in this project. Do not skip steps or │ attempt to do them out of order. │ Your goal is to make Storybook fully functional in this project by │ analyzing the codebase, │ configuring the preview with the right decorators, and writing stories │ for some components. │ After each created story, run Vitest to verify it renders. │ If the test fails, read the error, fix the issue, and re-run until it │ passes before moving on. │ - Copy real patterns from the codebase │ - Keep the app code unchanged │ - Put the default setup in `.storybook/preview.tsx` │ - Keep app mocking and runtime setup in `.storybook/preview.tsx`, not │ in the stories │ ### Storybook Documentation Reference │ Use the following references to look up Storybook APIs, concepts, or │ examples: │ - Full docs index: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/llms.txthttps://storybook.js.org/llms.txt]8;; │ - See code snippets only with codeOnly=true param e.g. │ ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-stories.md?renderer=react&language=js&codeOnly=truehttps://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-stories.md?renderer=react&language=js&codeOnly=true]8;; │ Key documentation pages for this task: │ - Writing stories: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-stories.md?renderer=react&language=jshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-stories.md...]8;; │ - Decorators: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-stories/decorators.md?renderer=react&language=jshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-stories/decorat...]8;; │ - Args: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-stories/args.md?renderer=react&language=jshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-stories/args.md?rende...]8;; │ - Play functions: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-stories/play-function.md?renderer=react&language=jshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-stories/pla...]8;; │ - Vitest integration: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-tests/vitest-plugin.md?renderer=react&language=jshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-tests/v...]8;; │ Fetch these URLs directly when you need guidance on Storybook APIs or │ patterns. │ ### Step 1: Analyze the codebase │ Read enough of the app to understand the full runtime environment │ before writing any stories. │ Do not stop at `main.tsx` or `App.tsx`. │ Follow imports into providers, pages, hooks, and shared components │ until you know: │ - which providers exist │ - which CSS files are injected │ - which queries fetch data │ - which browser-state reads happen │ - which portals and portal roots exist │ - which pages and components show the real usage patterns │ Example of what to copy: │ ```tsx │ // src/main.tsx │ import "./index.css"; │ import App from "./App"; │ import { SessionProvider } from "./contexts/SessionContext"; │ createRoot(document.getElementById("root")!).render( │ │ │ , │ ); │ ``` │ That means Storybook should copy: │ - the `index.css` import │ - the `SessionProvider` │ - the same provider order │ Example of tracing the app deeper: │ ```tsx │ // src/App.tsx │ function App() { │ const { products, loadMoreProducts } = useProducts(); │ const { currentUser, signOut } = useSession(); │ // ... │ } │ ``` │ ```ts │ // src/hooks/useProducts.ts │ const response = await fetch(apiBaseUrl + "/products?page=1"); │ ``` │ ```ts │ // src/hooks/useTheme.ts │ const savedTheme = localStorage.getItem("theme"); │ ``` │ That means the default Storybook setup should discover and prepare: │ - provider state │ - MSW handlers for queries │ - browser-state values that are actually read during render │ ### Step 2: Build one default app environment in preview │ Set up Storybook once so most stories work without story-specific │ setup. │ Start with the smallest faithful environment: │ - the real provider tree │ - the real root CSS │ - seeded browser state if the app reads it during render │ - MSW for network/data queries │ It is fine to seed browser state such as `localStorage`, │ `sessionStorage`, and cookies when the app reads them during render. │ Seed only the specific app-owned keys and values you need. │ Do not clear all `localStorage`, `sessionStorage`, or cookies, and do │ not reset Storybook's own state. │ Do not mock or redefine the browser runtime itself. │ The stories run in Vitest browser mode, so the real browser │ environment should already exist. │ ```tsx │ // .storybook/preview.tsx │ import type { Preview } from '@storybook/react-vite'; │ import MockDate from 'mockdate'; │ import '../src/index.css'; // import global styles │ import { SessionProvider } from '../src/contexts/SessionContext'; │ const preview: Preview = { │ decorators: [ │ (Story) => ( │ │ │ │ ), │ ], │ async beforeEach() { │ localStorage.setItem('theme', 'dark'); │ localStorage.setItem('sidebar:open', 'true'); │ MockDate.set('2024-04-01T12:00:00Z'); │ }, │ }; │ export default preview; │ ``` │ Use this same idea for: │ - providers │ - root CSS │ - browser state │ - dates, and if the app logic depends on them during render then │ always use `mockdate` │ Example with the `mockdate` package: │ ```tsx │ import type { Preview } from '@storybook/react-vite'; │ import MockDate from 'mockdate'; │ const preview: Preview = { │ async beforeEach() { │ MockDate.set('2024-04-01T12:00:00Z'); │ }, │ }; │ export default preview; │ ``` │ ### Step 3: Support portals with preview-body.html │ If the app uses portals, copy that setup into Storybook too. │ Look for patterns like: │ - `createPortal(...)` │ - modal, dialog, drawer, popover, tooltip, toast, or dropdown portal │ components │ - hard-coded roots such as `#portal-root`, `#modal-root`, │ `#drawer-root`, or `#toast-root` │ Example of what to copy: │ ```tsx │ // real component │ return createPortal(, │ document.getElementById("portal-root")!); │ ``` │ That means Storybook should create the same portal root in │ `.storybook/preview-body.html`: │ ```html │ │
│ ``` │ If the app uses multiple portal roots, create all of them there: │ ```html │ │
│
│
│ ``` │ If a library portals directly to `document.body`, do not add extra │ roots for it. │ Make sure the copied page shell, CSS, and layout still allow overlays, │ fixed positioning, and z-index stacking to render correctly. │ ### Step 4: Mock side effects globally │ All network/data queries should be handled by the default Storybook │ environment. │ - Always use `msw-storybook-addon` for query mocking. │ - If you introduce MSW, run `npx msw init ./public --save` to create │ the worker file. │ - Make sure Storybook serves `./public` as a static dir so │ `mockServiceWorker.js` is available. │ - Do not mock `fetch` directly. │ - Network/data queries should return deterministic mock data. │ - If you need to change dependencies, first check the lockfile and use │ that package manager for the change. │ Example of copying a real fetch pattern into shared handlers: │ ```ts │ // real app hook │ const response = await fetch( │ apiBaseUrl + │ "/products?" + │ new URLSearchParams({ │ page: "1", │ sort: "featured", │ }), │ ); │ ``` │ ```ts │ // .storybook/msw-handlers.ts │ import { http, HttpResponse } from "msw"; │ export const mswHandlers = { │ products: [ │ http.get("]8;;https://api.example.com/products",https://api.example.com/products",]8;; │ () => │ HttpResponse.json({ │ items: [ │ { │ id: "product-1", │ name: "Example product", │ description: "Mock product description", │ imageUrl: │ "]8;;https://images.example.com/product.jpg",https://images.example.com/product.jpg",]8;; │ price: 42, │ }, │ ], │ }), │ ), │ ], │ }; │ ``` │ ```tsx │ // .storybook/preview.tsx │ import type { Preview } from '@storybook/react-vite'; │ import { initialize, mswLoader } from 'msw-storybook-addon'; │ import { mswHandlers } from './msw-handlers'; │ initialize({ │ onUnhandledRequest: 'bypass', │ }); │ const preview: Preview = { │ loaders: [mswLoader], │ parameters: { │ msw: { │ handlers: mswHandlers, │ }, │ }, │ }; │ export default preview; │ ``` │ ```ts │ // .storybook/main.ts │ import type { StorybookConfig } from "@storybook/react-vite"; │ const config: StorybookConfig = { │ staticDirs: ["../public"], │ }; │ export default config; │ ``` │ Keep these mocks global. │ Do not put fetch mocks in individual stories. │ Only add handlers for requests that the shared preview setup or the │ stories actually use. │ Do not add catch-all handlers that can hide unrelated failures. │ If the defaults are not enough, improve the shared default setup │ instead. │ Seed browser state when needed, but do not mock `window`, `document`, │ `navigator`, observers, or similar runtime APIs. │ The only exception is `mockdate` when date-based rendering exists. │ ### Step 5: Write stories │ Try to find around 10 good candidate components for story files. │ Write colocated stories for top-level components, from low-level │ reusable components up to page components. │ Write up to 10 story files, or fewer only if the codebase clearly has │ fewer meaningful targets. │ The stories should use JSX copied from real usage patterns in: │ - pages │ - app shells │ - routes │ - tests │ - existing feature code │ As a rule of thumb, each story file should have around 3 story exports │ when the component or page has enough meaningful states. │ It can have more when the real usage supports it, up to 10 story │ exports in one file. │ Always show all imports explicitly in story and preview files. │ Do not rely on omitted or implied imports in examples or generated │ code. │ #### Story tags │ Every story meta must include the `ai-generated` tag to identify │ AI-created stories: │ ```tsx │ import type { Meta, StoryObj } from '@storybook/react-vite'; │ import { expect } from 'storybook/test'; │ import { SomeComponent } from './SomeComponent'; │ const meta = { │ component: SomeComponent, │ tags: ['ai-generated'], │ } satisfies Meta; │ export default meta; │ type Story = StoryObj; │ export const Default: Story = { │ render: () => , │ play: async ({ canvas }) => { │ await expect(canvas.getByRole('button')).toBeVisible(); │ }, │ }; │ ``` │ If a story could not be fully fixed after the self-healing loop (the │ test still fails │ or the rendering is incomplete), add the `needs-work` tag alongside │ `ai-generated`: │ ```ts │ const meta = { │ component: SomeComponent, │ tags: ['ai-generated', 'needs-work'], │ } satisfies Meta; │ ``` │ Keep app mocking and runtime setup in preview, not in the stories. │ Do not build large story-specific harnesses. │ Do not write story files for subcomponents, hooks, contexts, or │ helpers. │ Do not create new application components. │ Do not add a custom `title`. │ Do not stop after only a few easy targets if the codebase has more │ meaningful components or pages available. │ ### Step 6: Write a play function for every story │ Every named story export must have a `play` function. │ The `play` function is not optional, even for simple stories. │ The purpose of the `play` function is to prove that the story actually │ works in the copied Storybook environment: │ - the story renders something real and non-empty │ - the decorators provide the needed context │ - the CSS is applied well enough for the intended state to be visible │ - the MSW mocks or seeded browser state are actually being used │ - important interactions, async loading states, and portals behave │ correctly │ Use `play` functions to verify behavior, not just to click around. │ A story without assertions is incomplete. │ Use tools from `storybook/test` such as: │ - `expect` │ - `waitFor` │ Prefer `canvas` and `userEvent` from the `play` context. │ Do not destructure `canvasElement` just to create `const canvas = │ within(canvasElement)`. │ Do not import `userEvent` from `storybook/test`; use `userEvent` from │ the `play` context instead. │ Only use `canvasElement.ownerDocument` when you need to query outside │ the canvas, such as for portals. │ Example: │ ```tsx │ import type { StoryObj } from "@storybook/react-vite"; │ export const FilledForm: Story = { │ play: async ({ canvas, userEvent }) => { │ const emailInput = canvas.getByLabelText("email", { │ selector: "input", │ }); │ await userEvent.type(emailInput, "example-email@email.com", { │ delay: 100, │ }); │ const passwordInput = canvas.getByLabelText("password", { │ selector: "input", │ }); │ await userEvent.type(passwordInput, "ExamplePassword", { │ delay: 100, │ }); │ const submitButton = canvas.getByRole("button"); │ await userEvent.click(submitButton); │ }, │ }; │ ``` │ The assertions should match the real pattern you copied: │ - for provider-backed stories, assert the provider-dependent UI │ appears correctly │ - for mocked-data stories, wait for the mocked data to appear and │ assert on it │ - for CSS-sensitive states, assert on visibility, text layout, │ class-driven states, or meaningful computed styles │ - for routing or navigation stories, assert the routed state or │ navigation outcome │ - for portal stories, query from `canvasElement.ownerDocument` when │ the UI renders outside the canvas │ Examples of useful checks: │ - a themed button has the expected label and is visibly enabled or │ disabled │ - a modal opened through a decorator or provider is visible in the │ portal root │ - mocked API data appears in the page instead of a loading spinner │ forever │ - a selected tab actually shows the selected panel │ - a toast, alert, or badge has the expected accessible text and visual │ state │ - a CSS class or computed style confirms the real state that matters │ ### Step 7: Cover the patterns you found │ Write stories for the real patterns in the codebase, for example: │ - a low-level reusable component in real JSX usage │ - a provider-backed component │ - a browser-state-backed component │ - a fetched-data component │ - a real page component │ Use `App.tsx` to inspect the real provider tree and usage patterns, │ but do not make a story for `App` when the codebase has actual page │ components. │ Example page story: │ ```tsx │ import type { Meta, StoryObj } from '@storybook/react-vite'; │ import { expect } from 'storybook/test'; │ import { ProductPage } from './ProductPage'; │ const meta = { │ component: ProductPage, │ tags: ['ai-generated'], │ } satisfies Meta; │ export default meta; │ type Story = StoryObj; │ export const Default: Story = { │ render: () => , │ play: async ({ canvas }) => { │ await expect( │ canvas.getByRole('heading', { name: /products/i }), │ ).toBeVisible(); │ }, │ }; │ ``` │ ### Step 8: Verify both rendering and types │ As you work, verify the stories with Vitest: │ ```bash │ npx vitest --project storybook │ ``` │ Also verify types so you catch missing required props, broken imports, │ and preview typing issues. Run the same TypeScript command the project │ itself uses. │ ```bash │ │ ``` │ After verification passes, review every changed file and remove │ anything that is not needed for the final solution, especially debug │ fixes, overly broad mocks, unnecessary dependencies, and eval │ artifacts. │ Keep iterating until: │ - every story you wrote passes │ - every story you wrote has a meaningful passing `play` function │ - the changed stories and preview setup pass the project's real │ TypeScript check │ - the rendered output looks sensible │ - the default global mocked environment is strong enough that stories │ do not need manual fetch overrides │ - stories no longer fail because the shared preview setup and story │ JSX are fixed │ - all passing stories have `tags: ['ai-generated']` in their meta │ - any stories that still need work have `tags: ['ai-generated', │ 'needs-work']` in their meta

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→export const useKey = (key: string, keyDownCb = () => {}, keyUpCb = () => {}): boolean => {
     4→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false)
     5→  const match = useCallback((event: any) => key.toLowerCase() === event.key.toLowerCase(), [key])
     6→
     7→  const onKeyDown = useCallback(
     8→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
     9→      if (match(event)) {
    10→        setPressed(true)
    11→        keyDownCb()
    12→      }
    13→    },
    14→    [keyDownCb, match]
    15→  )
    16→
    17→  const onKeyUp = useCallback(
    18→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
    19→      if (match(event)) {
    20→        setPressed(false)
    21→        keyUpCb()
    22→      }
    23→    },
    24→    [keyUpCb, match]
    25→  )
    26→
    27→  useEffect(() => {
    28→    globalThis.addEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    29→    globalThis.addEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    30→
    31→    return () => {
    32→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    33→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    34→    }
    35→  }, [key, onKeyDown, onKeyUp])
    36→
    37→  return pressed
    38→}
    39→

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}

1→import { useRef, useEffect } from 'react'

     3→import { createPortal } from 'react-dom'
     4→
     5→type Props = {
     6→  selector: string
     7→}
     8→
     9→export const Portal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<Props>> = ({ children, selector }) => {
    10→  const ref = useRef<Element | null>(null)
    11→
    12→  useEffect(() => {
    13→    const element = document.querySelector<Element>(selector)
    14→    if (element) {
    15→      ref.current = element
    16→    }
    17→  }, [selector])
    18→
    19→  return ref.current ? createPortal(children, ref.current) : null
    20→}

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information about the codebase. Let me create a detailed report:

## Codebase Analysis Report: Course App (Mealdrop)

### 1. Entry Point
**File:** `/src/index.tsx`
- Entry point renders the React app into the `#root` DOM element using React 19
- Wraps the app with `StrictMode`
- Initializes and registers the service worker (currently unregistered)

### 2. App.tsx and Provider Tree
**File:** `/src/App.tsx`

The complete provider hierarchy is:
```
StrictMode
├── BrowserRouter (react-router-dom)
│   └── Provider (Redux - react-redux)
│       └── ThemeProvider (styled-components)
│           ├── GlobalStyle (styled-components global styles)
│           └── AppRoutes (component tree)
```

**Providers Used:**
1. **React Router DOM** - `BrowserRouter` for client-side routing
2. **Redux** - `Provider` with configured store for state management
3. **Styled Components** - `ThemeProvider` for theme management (light/dark mode)
4. **Dark Mode Hook** - `use-dark-mode` (applied at App level to switch themes)

### 3. All Providers and Contexts

| Provider | Library | Purpose | Config |
|----------|---------|---------|--------|
| BrowserRouter | react-router-dom v7.2.0 | Client routing | Basic setup, no custom route guards |
| Redux Store Provider | react-redux v9.2.0, @reduxjs/toolkit v2.6.0 | Global state management | Configured with cart and order slices |
| ThemeProvider | styled-components v6.1.15 | Theme switching | Light/dark themes |
| useDarkMode Hook | use-dark-mode v2.3.1 | Dark mode state | Returns `{value, toggle}` |
| GlobalStyle | styled-components | CSS reset + global styles | Applied globally via createGlobalStyle |

**No React Context providers detected** - All state management uses Redux.

### 4. CSS Files Globally Imported

**Global Styles in GlobalStyle.tsx:**
- **External Montserrat fonts** (400, 500, 700, 900 weights) from Google Fonts
- **External Hind fonts** (400, 500 weights) from Google Fonts
- **CSS Reset** - Custom reset CSS from `/src/styles/CSSReset.ts`
- **Third-party CSS:**
  - `react-multi-carousel/lib/styles.css` - Carousel styles
  - `react-loading-skeleton/dist/skeleton.css` - Skeleton loading styles

**CSS-in-JS**: All styling is via styled-components, no `.css` files imported globally. Storybook example CSS files exist but are not globally imported:
- `/src/stories/button.css`
- `/src/stories/header.css`
- `/src/stories/page.css`

### 5. All Pages/Route Components

**Routes defined in:** `/src/Routes.tsx`

| Route | Component | File Path |
|-------|-----------|-----------|
| `/` | HomePage | `/src/pages/HomePage/HomePage.tsx` |
| `/categories` | CategoryListPage | `/src/pages/CategoryListPage/CategoryListPage.tsx` |
| `/categories/:id` | CategoryDetailPage | `/src/pages/CategoryDetailPage/CategoryDetailPage.tsx` |
| `/restaurants/:id` | RestaurantDetailPage | `/src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage/RestaurantDetailPage.tsx` |
| `/checkout` | CheckoutPage | `/src/pages/CheckoutPage/CheckoutPage.tsx` |
| `/success` | SuccessPage | `/src/pages/SuccessPage/SuccessPage.tsx` |

**Layout Template:** All pages use `/src/templates/PageTemplate.tsx` which supports three modes:
- `default` - Header + Container + Footer
- `sticky-header` - Header (sticky) + Container + Footer
- `basic` - Logo-only Header + Container (no footer)

### 6. Reusable Components

**Directory:** `/src/components/`

**UI Components:**
- **Badge** - Badge/label component
- **Button** - Primary/secondary/clear button variants
- **Icon & IconButton** - SVG icons and icon-based buttons
- **Logo** - Branded logo
- **Spinner** - Loading spinner
- **ErrorBlock** - Error message display
- **Breadcrumb** - Navigation breadcrumb
- **Review** - Review/rating display
- **AnimatedIllustration** - Animated Lottie illustrations

**Layout Components:**
- **Header** - Navigation header with shopping cart integration
- **Footer** - Footer component
- **PageSection** - Page section wrapper
- **Sidebar** - Side navigation
- **TopBanner** - Banner component

**Complex Components:**
- **Modal** - Modal dialog (uses Portal, CSSTransition, useKey, useLockBodyScroll)
- **ShoppingCart** - Shopping cart container
  - `ShoppingCartMenu` - Cart menu overlay
  - `OrderSummary` - Order summary display
  - `ShoppingCartItem` - Individual cart item
- **RestaurantCard** - Restaurant display card (with basic/unstyled variants)
- **Category** - Category display component

**Form/Typography:**
- **Input** - Form input component
- **Select** - Select dropdown component
- **Body** - Body text typography component
- **Heading** - Heading typography component (h1-h4 levels)

**Special Components:**
- **Portal** - React Portal wrapper for modals (`src/components/Portal.tsx`)

### 7. Data-Fetching Hooks and Browser API Usage

**File:** `/src/api/hooks.ts`

Three custom hooks for data fetching:

```typescript
useFetchRestaurants()       // Fetch all restaurants
useFetchRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId: string)  // Fetch by category
useFetchRestaurant(id: string)      // Fetch single restaurant
```

Each returns: `{ status: string, data/restaurants/restaurant: T[], retryRequest: () => void }`

**API Configuration:**
- **Base URL:** `https://mealdrop.netlify.app/.netlify/functions/restaurants`
- **Client:** Axios with caching (skipped in test/storybook environments)
- **Error Handling:** Status codes returned as strings (e.g., "404", "500")
- **Conditional Logic:** `/src/api/conditional-logic.ts` provides mock API that uses stub data when `STORYBOOK` or `NODE_ENV=test`

**Browser API Hooks:**

| Hook | Purpose | Browser API | File |
|------|---------|-------------|------|
| `useLockBodyScroll(shouldLock)` | Prevent scrolling (for modals) | document.body.style.overflow | `/src/hooks/useBodyScrollLock.ts` |
| `useKey(key, keyDownCb, keyUpCb)` | Keyboard event listener | globalThis.addEventListener | `/src/hooks/useKeyboard.ts` |

**No localStorage/sessionStorage/cookies** - Not used in codebase.

### 8. Portal Usage

**Portal Component:** `/src/components/Portal.tsx`
- Custom wrapper around React's `createPortal`
- Takes a DOM selector string as prop
- Renders children into specified DOM node

**Usage:**
- **Modal Portal Root:** `<div id="modal" />` created in `AppRoutes.tsx` (Routes.tsx)
- **Modal Component Usage:** `<Portal selector="#modal">` in `/src/components/Modal/Modal.tsx`

### 9. Package Manager

**Package Manager:** Yarn v4.2.1
- Configuration: `.yarnrc.yml`
- Lock file: `yarn.lock` (342KB)
- Node modules location: `.yarn/` (PnP Yarn setup detected)

### 10. Storybook Configuration

**Configuration Files:**
- **Main Config:** `/storybook/main.ts`
- **Preview Config:** `/storybook/preview.tsx`

**Main Config Details:**
```typescript
Framework: @storybook/react-vite
Stories: [
  '../src/**/*.mdx',
  '../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)',
  './eval-support/*.mdx'
]
Addons: [
  '@chromatic-com/storybook',
  '@storybook/addon-vitest',
  '@storybook/addon-a11y',

]
```

**Preview Config:**
- Controls matchers for color and date properties
- A11y addon configured with 'todo' test mode

**Story Files Found:**
- `/src/stories/Button.stories.ts`
- `/src/stories/Header.stories.ts`
- `/src/stories/Page.stories.ts`

**Storybook Version:** Pre-release version (0.0.0-pr-34612-sha-d8de7419)

---

## Key Architectural Insights

1. **State Management:** Redux Toolkit with two slices: `cart` and `order`
2. **Styling:** 100% styled-components, no global CSS files
3. **Theme Support:** Light/dark mode via `use-dark-mode` hook and styled-components ThemeProvider
4. **Data Fetching:** Custom hooks with axios + caching, mock support for Storybook/tests
5. **Routing:** React Router v7 with 6 main routes

8. **No localStorage/sessionStorage:** Cart/order data stored in Redux only (session-based)

import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'
import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components'
import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'

import { store } from './app-state'
import { AppRoutes } from './Routes'
import { lightTheme, darkTheme } from './styles/theme'
import { GlobalStyle } from './styles/GlobalStyle'

export default function App() {
  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme

  return (
    <Router>
      <StoreProvider store={store}>
        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
          <GlobalStyle />
          <AppRoutes />
        </ThemeProvider>
      </StoreProvider>
    </Router>
  )
}

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}

    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}

    10→
    11→type ModalProps = {
    12→  isOpen: boolean
    13→  onClose: () => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→export const Modal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<ModalProps>> = ({
    17→  children,
    18→  isOpen,
    19→  onClose,
    20→}) => {
    21→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    22→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    23→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    24→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    25→
    26→  return (
    27→    <Portal selector="#modal">
    28→      <CSSTransition
    29→        in={isOpen}
    30→        timeout={300}
    31→        classNames="modal"
    32→        unmountOnExit
    33→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    34→      >
    35→        <ModalContent data-testid="modal" ref={contentRef}>
    36→          <TopBar>
    37→            <Button
    38→              data-testid="modal-close-btn"
    39→              onClick={onClose}
    40→              clear
    41→              round
    42→              icon="cross"
    43→              aria-label="close modal"
    44→              iconSize={16}
    45→            />
    46→          </TopBar>
    47→          {children}
    48→        </ModalContent>
    49→      </CSSTransition>
    50→
    51→      <CSSTransition
    52→        in={isOpen}
    53→        timeout={300}
    54→        classNames="backdrop"
    55→        unmountOnExit
    56→        nodeRef={backdropRef}
    57→      >
    58→        <Backdrop data-testid="modal-backdrop" ref={backdropRef} onClick={onClose} />
    59→      </CSSTransition>
    60→    </Portal>
    61→  )
    62→}
    63→

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me plan the stories I'll write:

1. **`Button.stories.tsx`** - Default, Large, Clear, WithIcon, Disabled
2. **`Badge.stories.tsx`** - Default
3. **`RestaurantCard.stories.tsx`** - Default, Closed, New, Skeleton
4. **`Header.stories.tsx`** - Default (empty cart), WithCart, LogoOnly


5. **`OrderSummary.stories.tsx`** - EmptyCart, WithItems
6. **`HomePage.stories.tsx`** - Default
7. **`CategoryListPage.stories.tsx`** - Default
8. **`CategoryDetailPage.stories.tsx`** - Default (with route params)
9. **`CheckoutPage.stories.tsx`** - Default (empty cart), WithItems
10. **`SuccessPage.stories.tsx`** - Default (empty order), WithOrder

The preview.tsx is already configured with MemoryRouter, Redux Provider with a fresh store for each story, ThemeProvider using the light theme, and GlobalStyle applied.

The preview.tsx doesn't need MSW since the API already has built-in mock support that detects the STORYBOOK environment variable. I need to add a modal portal root div to either preview.tsx as a decorator or in preview-body.html. For pages that require route parameters like CategoryDetailPage and RestaurantDetailPage, I'll need to wrap them with MemoryRouter and set initialEntries to the appropriate paths, though this adds some complexity at the page level. the preview's MemoryRouter already wraps everything, so nesting another one in the story could cause issues with React Router. The cleanest solution is to configure the route params through the global preview setup or use a per-story decorator that sets the initial entries properly.

Writing decorator pattern... I'm realizing the preview's MemoryRouter creates a routing context that story-level decorators can't easily override, so I need to either remove routing from the preview entirely and let each story handle it, or use a decorator approach that properly nests the routing contexts for stories that need params. The inner MemoryRouter will take precedence over the outer one from the preview, so `useParams` should work correctly in the stories. Now I'm going to write out the story files—I need to add the modal div to the preview body HTML first, then create stories for the Button, Badge, RestaurantCard, Header, OrderSummary, and the page components like HomePage, CategoryListPage, CategoryDetailPage, CheckoutPage, and SuccessPage. Let me check what's already in the preview-body.html file.

    18→  items: [],
    19→}
    20→
    21→const orderSlice = createSlice({
    22→  name: 'order',
    23→  initialState,
    24→  reducers: {
    25→    saveOrderAction: saveOrder,
    26→    clearOrderAction: clearOrder,
    27→  },
    28→})
    29→

    31→export const { actions, reducer } = orderSlice

    33→// Extract and export each action creator by name
    34→export const { saveOrderAction, clearOrderAction } = actions
    35→

1→import type { Meta, StoryObj } from '@storybook/react-vite'
     2→import { expect } from 'storybook/test'
     3→
     4→import { Button } from './Button'
     5→
     6→const meta = {
     7→  component: Button,
     8→  tags: ['ai-generated'],
     9→} satisfies Meta<typeof Button>
    10→
    11→export default meta
    12→type Story = StoryObj<typeof meta>
    13→
    14→export const Default: Story = {
    15→  render: () => <Button>Order now</Button>,
    16→  play: async ({ canvas }) => {
    17→    const button = canvas.getByRole('button', { name: 'Order now' })
    18→    await expect(button).toBeVisible()
    19→  },
    20→}
    21→
    22→export const Large: Story = {
    23→  render: () => <Button large>Checkout</Button>,
    24→  play: async ({ canvas }) => {
    25→    const button = canvas.getByRole('button', { name: 'Checkout' })
    26→    await expect(button).toBeVisible()
    27→  },
    28→}
    29→
    30→export const Clear: Story = {
    31→  render: () => <Button clear>Home</Button>,
    32→  play: async ({ canvas }) => {
    33→    const button = canvas.getByRole('button', { name: 'Home' })
    34→    await expect(button).toBeVisible()
    35→  },
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const WithIcon: Story = {
    39→  render: () => <Button icon="cart" aria-label="food cart" />,
    40→  play: async ({ canvas }) => {
    41→    const button = canvas.getByRole('button', { name: 'food cart' })
    42→    await expect(button).toBeVisible()
    43→  },
    44→}
    45→
    46→export const Disabled: Story = {
    47→  render: () => <Button disabled>Checkout</Button>,
    48→  play: async ({ canvas }) => {
    49→    const button = canvas.getByRole('button', { name: 'Checkout' })
    50→    await expect(button).toBeVisible()
    51→    await expect(button).toBeDisabled()
    52→  },
    53→}
    54→
    55→export const Round: Story = {
    56→  render: () => <Button round clear icon="cross" aria-label="close modal" iconSize={16} />,
    57→  play: async ({ canvas }) => {
    58→    const button = canvas.getByRole('button', { name: 'close modal' })
    59→    await expect(button).toBeVisible()
    60→  },
    61→}

The `msw-storybook-addon` package is not installed. Since the API already uses `MockedRestaurantsApi` in Storybook mode (via `import.meta.env.STORYBOOK`), we actually don't need MSW for the API calls. The `conditional-logic.ts` file already handles this.

The current preview.tsx was modified by a previous step to use `msw-storybook-addon`, but that package isn't installed. I need to either:


1. Install `msw-storybook-addon`
2. Or update preview.tsx to not use it (since we don't need it with the existing mock API)

Given that the app already has a built-in mock for Storybook/test environments, I'll go ahead and install `msw-storybook-addon` using yarn to resolve the issue.

content.thinking:
The issue is that the text "Pizza" appears in multiple elements:
1. In the paragraph text: "Feeling like having pizza? How about Sushi? ..."
2. In the category list as "Pizza"

The regex `/Pizza/i` matches both. I need to use a more specific matcher. Let me look at the CategoryListPage component to understand the structure.

The category items show "Pizza" in a span. Let me use `getByRole('link', { name: /pizza/i })` instead, or use `getAllByText` with a specific check, or use an exact match on the span.


Actually, I see the real problem now—the regex `/Pizza/i` is case-insensitive, so it's matching "pizza" in the paragraph text as well as "Pizza" in the category span. I should use `getByText('Pizza')` with exact matching to target only the capitalized version in the category list.

But wait, I'm second-guessing myself on how `getByText` works. Let me think through this more carefully. When I use `getByText('Pizza')`, it should do exact matching by default and be case-sensitive, so it would only find the span with "Pizza" capitalized, not the paragraph containing "pizza" lowercase. The paragraph's full text content isn't just "Pizza" anyway—it's the entire sentence.

So why did the earlier test fail when looking for "What's on the menu?"? Let me check what the actual heading text is...

The StyledHeading renders an h2 with that text, so it should be findable. Unless there's a character encoding issue—maybe the apostrophe in the test string is different from what's actually rendered, like a curly quote versus a straight quote.

I'll switch to using `getByRole('heading', { name: /menu/i })` for the heading and `getByText('Pizza')` for the category text, which should be more reliable.

All 45 tests pass across 17 story files. Here's a summary of what was done:

**Preview setup (`.storybook/preview.tsx`):**
- Installed `msw-storybook-addon` and configured MSW with handlers for the restaurant API
- Changed from `BrowserRouter` to `MemoryRouter` to support `parameters.reactRouter.initialEntries`
- Added `parameters.reactRouter.path` support to wrap stories in `<Routes><Route>` for `useParams()` 
- Added `parameters.preloadedState` support for pre-populating Redux state per story
- Creates a fresh Redux store per story (to isolate state between stories)

**Fixes made to story files:**
- `RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx` — removed nested `MemoryRouter` (which caused "You cannot render a <Router> inside another <Router>"), switched to `parameters.reactRouter`
- `CategoryListPage.stories.tsx` — used `getByRole('heading')` with regex + exact string `getByText('Pizza')` to avoid false matches from paragraph text

which providers exist │ - which CSS files are injected │ - which queries fetch data │ - which browser-state reads happen │ - which portals and portal roots exist │ - which pages and components show the real usage patterns │ Example of what to copy: │ ```tsx │ // src/main.tsx │ import "./index.css"; │ import App from "./App"; │ import { SessionProvider } from "./contexts/SessionContext"; │ createRoot(document.getElementById("root")!).render( │ │ │ , │ ); │ ``` │ That means Storybook should copy: │ - the `index.css` import │ - the `SessionProvider` │ - the same provider order │ Example of tracing the app deeper: │ ```tsx │ // src/App.tsx │ function App() { │ const { products, loadMoreProducts } = useProducts(); │ const { currentUser, signOut } = useSession(); │ // ... │ } │ ``` │ ```ts │ // src/hooks/useProducts.ts │ const response = await fetch(apiBaseUrl + "/products?page=1"); │ ``` │ ```ts │ // src/hooks/useTheme.ts │ const savedTheme = localStorage.getItem("theme"); │ ``` │ That means the default Storybook setup should discover and prepare: │ - provider state │ - MSW handlers for queries │ - browser-state values that are actually read during render │ ### Step 2: Build one default app environment in preview │ Set up Storybook once so most stories work without story-specific │ setup. │ Start with the smallest faithful environment: │ - the real provider tree │ - the real root CSS │ - seeded browser state if the app reads it during render │ - MSW for network/data queries │ It is fine to seed browser state such as `localStorage`, │ `sessionStorage`, and cookies when the app reads them during render. │ Seed only the specific app-owned keys and values you need. │ Do not clear all `localStorage`, `sessionStorage`, or cookies, and do │ not reset Storybook's own state. │ Do not mock or redefine the browser runtime itself. │ The stories run in Vitest browser mode, so the real browser │ environment should already exist. │ ```tsx │ // .storybook/preview.tsx │ import type { Preview } from '@storybook/react-vite'; │ import MockDate from 'mockdate'; │ import '../src/index.css'; // import global styles │ import { SessionProvider } from '../src/contexts/SessionContext'; │ const preview: Preview = { │ decorators: [ │ (Story) => ( │ │ │ │ ), │ ], │ async beforeEach() { │ localStorage.setItem('theme', 'dark'); │ localStorage.setItem('sidebar:open', 'true'); │ MockDate.set('2024-04-01T12:00:00Z'); │ }, │ }; │ export default preview; │ ``` │ Use this same idea for: │ - providers │ - root CSS │ - browser state │ - dates, and if the app logic depends on them during render then │ always use `mockdate` │ Example with the `mockdate` package: │ ```tsx │ import type { Preview } from '@storybook/react-vite'; │ import MockDate from 'mockdate'; │ const preview: Preview = { │ async beforeEach() { │ MockDate.set('2024-04-01T12:00:00Z'); │ }, │ }; │ export default preview; │ ``` │ ### Step 3: Support portals with preview-body.html │ If the app uses portals, copy that setup into Storybook too. │ Look for patterns like: │ - 1→import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'
     2→import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components'
     3→import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
     4→import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'
     5→
     6→import { store } from './app-state'
     7→import { AppRoutes } from './Routes'
     8→import { lightTheme, darkTheme } from './styles/theme'
     9→import { GlobalStyle } from './styles/GlobalStyle'
    10→
    11→export default function App() {
    12→  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
    13→  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme
    14→
    15→  return (
    16→    <Router>
    17→      <StoreProvider store={store}>
    18→        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
    19→          <GlobalStyle />
    20→          <AppRoutes />
    21→        </ThemeProvider>
    22→      </StoreProvider>
    23→    </Router>
    24→  )
    25→}
    26→

content.content:
1→import { useLayoutEffect } from 'react'
     2→import { Routes, Route, useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'
     3→
     4→import { CategoryListPage } from './pages/CategoryListPage'
     5→import { CategoryDetailPage } from './pages/CategoryDetailPage'
     6→import { RestaurantDetailPage } from './pages/RestaurantDetailPage'
     7→import { HomePage } from './pages/HomePage'
     8→import { CheckoutPage } from './pages/CheckoutPage'
     9→import { SuccessPage } from './pages/SuccessPage'
    10→
    11→export const AppRoutes: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<unknown>> = ({ children }) => {
    12→  const location = useLocation()
    13→  // Scroll to top when a path changes
    14→  useLayoutEffect(() => {
    15→    window.scrollTo(0, 0)
    16→  }, [location.pathname])
    17→
    18→  return (
    19→    <>
    20→      <div id="modal" />
    21→      <Routes>
    22→        <Route path="/categories" element={<CategoryListPage />} />
    23→        <Route path="/categories/:id" element={<CategoryDetailPage />} />
    24→        <Route path="/restaurants/:id" element={<RestaurantDetailPage />} />
    25→        <Route path="/checkout" element={<CheckoutPage />} />
    26→        <Route path="/success" element={<SuccessPage />} />
    27→        <Route path="/" element={<HomePage />} />
    28→        {children}
    29→      </Routes>
    30→    </>
    31→  )
    32→}
    33→

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→export const useKey = (key: string, keyDownCb = () => {}, keyUpCb = () => {}): boolean => {
     4→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false)
     5→  const match = useCallback((event: any) => key.toLowerCase() === event.key.toLowerCase(), [key])
     6→
     7→  const onKeyDown = useCallback(
     8→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
     9→      if (match(event)) {
    10→        setPressed(true)
    11→        keyDownCb()
    12→      }
    13→    },
    14→    [keyDownCb, match]
    15→  )
    16→
    17→  const onKeyUp = useCallback(
    18→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
    19→      if (match(event)) {
    20→        setPressed(false)
    21→        keyUpCb()
    22→      }
    23→    },
    24→    [keyUpCb, match]
    25→  )
    26→
    27→  useEffect(() => {
    28→    globalThis.addEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    29→    globalThis.addEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    30→
    31→    return () => {
    32→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    33→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    34→    }
    35→  }, [key, onKeyDown, onKeyUp])
    36→
    37→  return pressed
    38→}
    39→

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}
   101→

1→import { useRef, useEffect } from 'react'
     2→import * as React from 'react'
     3→import { createPortal } from 'react-dom'
     4→
     5→type Props = {
     6→  selector: string
     7→}
     8→
     9→export const Portal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<Props>> = ({ children, selector }) => {
    10→  const ref = useRef<Element | null>(null)
    11→
    12→  useEffect(() => {
    13→    const element = document.querySelector<Element>(selector)
    14→    if (element) {
    15→      ref.current = element
    16→    }
    17→  }, [selector])
    18→
    19→  return ref.current ? createPortal(children, ref.current) : null
    20→}
    21→

1→import { createSlice, CaseReducer, PayloadAction } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'
     2→
     3→import { CartItem } from '../cart'
     4→
     5→export type OrderState = {
     6→  items: CartItem[]
     7→}
     8→
     9→const saveOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState, PayloadAction<CartItem[]>> = (state, action) => {
    10→  state.items = action.payload
    11→}
    12→
    13→const clearOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState> = (state) => {
    14→  state.items = []
    15→}
    16→
    17→const initialState: OrderState = {
    18→  items: [],
    19→}
    20→
    21→const orderSlice = createSlice({
    22→  name: 'order',
    23→  initialState,
    24→  reducers: {
    25→    saveOrderAction: saveOrder,
    26→    clearOrderAction: clearOrder,
    27→  },
    28→})
    29→
    30→// Extract the action creators object and the reducer
    31→export const { actions, reducer } = orderSlice
    32→
    33→// Extract and export each action creator by name
    34→export const { saveOrderAction, clearOrderAction } = actions
    35→
    36→// Export the reducer, either as a default or named export
    37→export default reducer

     3→
     4→import { Portal } from '../Portal'
     5→import { useKey } from '../../hooks/useKeyboard'
     6→import { Button } from '../Button'
     7→import { useLockBodyScroll } from '../../hooks/useBodyScrollLock'

    10→
    11→type ModalProps = {
    12→  isOpen: boolean
    13→  onClose: () => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→export const Modal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<ModalProps>> = ({
    17→  children,
    18→  isOpen,
    19→  onClose,
    20→}) => {
    21→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    22→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    23→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    24→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    25→
    26→  return (
    27→    <Portal selector="#modal">
    28→      <CSSTransition
    29→        in={isOpen}
    30→        timeout={300}
    31→        classNames="modal"
    32→        unmountOnExit
    33→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    34→      >
    35→        <ModalContent data-testid="modal" ref={contentRef}>
    36→          <TopBar>
    37→            <Button
    38→              data-testid="modal-close-btn"
    39→              onClick={onClose}
    40→              clear

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import styled from 'styled-components'
     3→
     4→import { Header, HeaderComponent } from '../components/Header/Header'
     5→import { Footer } from '../components/Footer/Footer'
     6→
     7→const Container = styled.div`
     8→  min-height: calc(100vh - 200px);
     9→`
    10→
    11→type PageTemplateProperties = {
    12→  type?: 'default' | 'sticky-header' | 'basic'
    13→}
    14→
    15→export const PageTemplate: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<PageTemplateProperties>> = ({
    16→  type = 'default',
    17→  children,
    18→}) => {
    19→  switch (type) {
    20→    case 'sticky-header': {
    21→      return (
    22→        <>
    23→          <Header sticky />
    24→          <Container>{children}</Container>
    25→          <Footer />
    26→        </>
    27→      )
    28→    }
    29→    case 'basic': {
    30→      return (
    31→        <>
    32→          <HeaderComponent logoOnly />
    33→          <Container>{children}</Container>
    34→        </>
    35→      )
    36→    }
    37→  }
    38→
    39→  return (
    40→    <>
    41→      <Header />
    42→      <Container>{children}</Container>
    43→      <Footer />
    44→    </>
    45→  )
    46→}

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information about the codebase. Let me create a detailed report:

## Codebase Analysis Report: Course App (Mealdrop)

### 1. Entry Point
**File:** `/src/index.tsx`
- Entry point renders the React app into the `#root` DOM element using React 19
- Wraps the app with `StrictMode`
- Initializes and registers the service worker (currently unregistered)

### 2. App.tsx and Provider Tree
**File:** `/src/App.tsx`

The complete provider hierarchy is:
```
StrictMode
├── BrowserRouter (react-router-dom)
│   └── Provider (Redux - react-redux)
│       └── ThemeProvider (styled-components)
│           ├── GlobalStyle (styled-components global styles)
│           └── AppRoutes (component tree)
```

**Providers Used:**
1. **React Router DOM** - `BrowserRouter` for client-side routing
2. **Redux** - `Provider` with configured store for state management
3. **Styled Components** - `ThemeProvider` for theme management (light/dark mode)
4. **Dark Mode Hook** - `use-dark-mode` (applied at App level to switch themes)

### 3. All Providers and Contexts

| Provider | Library | Purpose | Config |
|----------|---------|---------|--------|
| BrowserRouter | react-router-dom v7.2.0 | Client routing | Basic setup, no custom route guards |
| Redux Store Provider | react-redux v9.2.0, @reduxjs/toolkit v2.6.0 | Global state management | Configured with cart and order slices |
| ThemeProvider | styled-components v6.1.15 | Theme switching | Light/dark themes |
| useDarkMode Hook | use-dark-mode v2.3.1 | Dark mode state | Returns `{value, toggle}` |
| GlobalStyle | styled-components | CSS reset + global styles | Applied globally via createGlobalStyle |

**No React Context providers detected** - All state management uses Redux.

### 4. CSS Files Globally Imported

**Global Styles in GlobalStyle.tsx:**
- **External Montserrat fonts** (400, 500, 700, 900 weights) from Google Fonts
- **External Hind fonts** (400, 500 weights) from Google Fonts
- **CSS Reset** - Custom reset CSS from `/src/styles/CSSReset.ts`
- **Third-party CSS:**
  - `react-multi-carousel/lib/styles.css` - Carousel styles
  - `react-loading-skeleton/dist/skeleton.css` - Skeleton loading styles

**CSS-in-JS**: All styling is via styled-components, no `.css` files imported globally. Storybook example CSS files exist but are not globally imported:
- `/src/stories/button.css`
- `/src/stories/header.css`
- `/src/stories/page.css`

### 5. All Pages/Route Components

**Routes defined in:** `/src/Routes.tsx`

| Route | Component | File Path |
|-------|-----------|-----------|
| `/` | HomePage | `/src/pages/HomePage/HomePage.tsx` |
| `/categories` | CategoryListPage | `/src/pages/CategoryListPage/CategoryListPage.tsx` |
| `/categories/:id` | CategoryDetailPage | `/src/pages/CategoryDetailPage/CategoryDetailPage.tsx` |
| `/restaurants/:id` | RestaurantDetailPage | `/src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage/RestaurantDetailPage.tsx` |
| `/checkout` | CheckoutPage | `/src/pages/CheckoutPage/CheckoutPage.tsx` |
| `/success` | SuccessPage | `/src/pages/SuccessPage/SuccessPage.tsx` |

**Layout Template:** All pages use `/src/templates/PageTemplate.tsx` which supports three modes:
- `default` - Header + Container + Footer
- `sticky-header` - Header (sticky) + Container + Footer
- `basic` - Logo-only Header + Container (no footer)

### 6. Reusable Components

**Directory:** `/src/components/`

**UI Components:**
- **Badge** - Badge/label component
- **Button** - Primary/secondary/clear button variants
- **Icon & IconButton** - SVG icons and icon-based buttons
- **Logo** - Branded logo
- **Spinner** - Loading spinner
- **ErrorBlock** - Error message display
- **Breadcrumb** - Navigation breadcrumb
- **Review** - Review/rating display
- **AnimatedIllustration** - Animated Lottie illustrations

**Layout Components:**
- **Header** - Navigation header with shopping cart integration
- **Footer** - Footer component
- **PageSection** - Page section wrapper
- **Sidebar** - Side navigation
- **TopBanner** - Banner component

**Complex Components:**
- **Modal** - Modal dialog (uses Portal, CSSTransition, useKey, useLockBodyScroll)
- **ShoppingCart** - Shopping cart container
  - `ShoppingCartMenu` - Cart menu overlay
  - `OrderSummary` - Order summary display
  - `ShoppingCartItem` - Individual cart item
- **RestaurantCard** - Restaurant display card (with basic/unstyled variants)
- **Category** - Category display component

**Form/Typography:**
- **Input** - Form input component
- **Select** - Select dropdown component
- **Body** - Body text typography component
- **Heading** - Heading typography component (h1-h4 levels)

**Special Components:**
- **Portal** - React Portal wrapper for modals (`src/components/Portal.tsx`)

### 7. Data-Fetching Hooks and Browser API Usage

**File:** `/src/api/hooks.ts`

Three custom hooks for data fetching:

```typescript
useFetchRestaurants()       // Fetch all restaurants
useFetchRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId: string)  // Fetch by category
useFetchRestaurant(id: string)      // Fetch single restaurant
```

Each returns: `{ status: string, data/restaurants/restaurant: T[], retryRequest: () => void }`

**API Configuration:**
- **Base URL:** `https://mealdrop.netlify.app/.netlify/functions/restaurants`
- **Client:** Axios with caching (skipped in test/storybook environments)
- **Error Handling:** Status codes returned as strings (e.g., "404", "500")
- **Conditional Logic:** `/src/api/conditional-logic.ts` provides mock API that uses stub data when `STORYBOOK` or `NODE_ENV=test`

**Browser API Hooks:**

| Hook | Purpose | Browser API | File |
|------|---------|-------------|------|
| `useLockBodyScroll(shouldLock)` | Prevent scrolling (for modals) | document.body.style.overflow | `/src/hooks/useBodyScrollLock.ts` |
| `useKey(key, keyDownCb, keyUpCb)` | Keyboard event listener | globalThis.addEventListener | `/src/hooks/useKeyboard.ts` |

**No localStorage/sessionStorage/cookies** - Not used in codebase.

### 8. Portal Usage

**Portal Component:** `/src/components/Portal.tsx`
- Custom wrapper around React's `createPortal`
- Takes a DOM selector string as prop
- Renders children into specified DOM node

**Usage:**
- **Modal Portal Root:** `<div id="modal" />` created in `AppRoutes.tsx` (Routes.tsx)
- **Modal Component Usage:** `<Portal selector="#modal">` in `/src/components/Modal/Modal.tsx`

### 9. Package Manager

**Package Manager:** Yarn v4.2.1
- Configuration: `.yarnrc.yml`
- Lock file: `yarn.lock` (342KB)
- Node modules location: `.yarn/` (PnP Yarn setup detected)

### 10. Storybook Configuration

**Configuration Files:**
- **Main Config:** `/storybook/main.ts`
- **Preview Config:** `/storybook/preview.tsx`

**Main Config Details:**
```typescript
Framework: @storybook/react-vite
Stories: [
  '../src/**/*.mdx',
  '../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)',
  './eval-support/*.mdx'
]
Addons: [
  '@chromatic-com/storybook',
  '@storybook/addon-vitest',
  '@storybook/addon-a11y',
  '@storybook/addon-docs',
  '@storybook/addon-onboarding'
]
```

**Preview Config:**
- Controls matchers for color and date properties
- A11y addon configured with 'todo' test mode

**Story Files Found:**
- `/src/stories/Button.stories.ts`
- `/src/stories/Header.stories.ts`
- `/src/stories/Page.stories.ts`

**Storybook Version:** Pre-release version (0.0.0-pr-34612-sha-d8de7419)

---

## Key Architectural Insights

1. **State Management:** Redux Toolkit with two slices: `cart` and `order`
2. **Styling:** 100% styled-components, no global CSS files
3. **Theme Support:** Light/dark mode via `use-dark-mode` hook and styled-components ThemeProvider
4. **Data Fetching:** Custom hooks with axios + caching, mock support for Storybook/tests
5. **Routing:** React Router v7 with 6 main routes
6. **Modal System:** Portal-based with keyboard escape handling and body scroll locking
7. **No Context API:** Pure Redux for all shared state
8. **No localStorage/sessionStorage:** Cart/order data stored in Redux only (session-based)

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}
   101→

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import { CSSTransition } from 'react-transition-group'
     3→
     4→import { Portal } from '../Portal'
     5→import { useKey } from '../../hooks/useKeyboard'
     6→import { Button } from '../Button'
     7→import { useLockBodyScroll } from '../../hooks/useBodyScrollLock'
     8→
     9→import { ModalContent, TopBar, Backdrop } from './Modal.styles'
    10→
    11→type ModalProps = {
    12→  isOpen: boolean
    13→  onClose: () => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→export const Modal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<ModalProps>> = ({
    17→  children,
    18→  isOpen,
    19→  onClose,
    20→}) => {
    21→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    22→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    23→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    24→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    25→
    26→  return (
    27→    <Portal selector="#modal">
    28→      <CSSTransition
    29→        in={isOpen}
    30→        timeout={300}
    31→        classNames="modal"
    32→        unmountOnExit
    33→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    34→      >
    35→        <ModalContent data-testid="modal" ref={contentRef}>
    36→          <TopBar>
    37→            <Button
    38→              data-testid="modal-close-btn"
    39→              onClick={onClose}
    40→              clear
    41→              round
    42→              icon="cross"
    43→              aria-label="close modal"
    44→              iconSize={16}
    45→            />
    46→          </TopBar>
    47→          {children}
    48→        </ModalContent>
    49→      </CSSTransition>
    50→
    51→      <CSSTransition
    52→        in={isOpen}
    53→        timeout={300}
    54→        classNames="backdrop"
    55→        unmountOnExit
    56→        nodeRef={backdropRef}
    57→      >
    58→        <Backdrop data-testid="modal-backdrop" ref={backdropRef} onClick={onClose} />
    59→      </CSSTransition>
    60→    </Portal>
    61→  )
    62→}
    63→

1→import { useRef, useEffect } from 'react'
     2→import * as React from 'react'
     3→import { createPortal } from 'react-dom'
     4→
     5→type Props = {
     6→  selector: string
     7→}
     8→
     9→export const Portal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<Props>> = ({ children, selector }) => {
    10→  const ref = useRef<Element | null>(null)
    11→
    12→  useEffect(() => {
    13→    const element = document.querySelector<Element>(selector)
    14→    if (element) {
    15→      ref.current = element
    16→    }
    17→  }, [selector])
    18→
    19→  return ref.current ? createPortal(children, ref.current) : null
    20→}
    21→

1→import { createSlice, CaseReducer, PayloadAction } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'
     2→
     3→import { CartItem } from '../cart'
     4→
     5→export type OrderState = {
     6→  items: CartItem[]
     7→}
     8→
     9→const saveOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState, PayloadAction<CartItem[]>> = (state, action) => {
    10→  state.items = action.payload
    11→}
    12→
    13→const clearOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState> = (state) => {
    14→  state.items = []
    15→}
    16→
    17→const initialState: OrderState = {
    18→  items: [],
    19→}
    20→
    21→const orderSlice = createSlice({
    22→  name: 'order',
    23→  initialState,
    24→  reducers: {
    25→    saveOrderAction: saveOrder,
    26→    clearOrderAction: clearOrder,
    27→  },
    28→})
    29→
    30→// Extract the action creators object and the reducer
    31→export const { actions, reducer } = orderSlice
    32→
    33→// Extract and export each action creator by name
    34→export const { saveOrderAction, clearOrderAction } = actions
    35→
    36→// Export the reducer, either as a default or named export
    37→export default reducer
    38→

Now I need to fix the preview to:
1. Support `parameters.preloadedState` for Redux

content.text:
All 45 tests pass across 17 story files. Here's a summary of what was done:

**Preview setup (`.storybook/preview.tsx`):**
- Installed `msw-storybook-addon` and configured MSW with handlers for the restaurant API
- Changed from `BrowserRouter` to `MemoryRouter` to support `parameters.reactRouter.initialEntries`
- Added `parameters.reactRouter.path` support to wrap stories in `<Routes><Route>` for `useParams()` 
- Added `parameters.preloadedState` support for pre-populating Redux state per story
- Creates a fresh Redux store per story (to isolate state between stories)

**Fixes made to story files:**
- `RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx` — removed nested `MemoryRouter` (which caused "You cannot render a <Router> inside another <Router>"), switched to `parameters.reactRouter`
- `CategoryListPage.stories.tsx` — used `getByRole('heading')` with regex + exact string `getByText('Pizza')` to avoid false matches from paragraph text
